Add this to the top of vision.md:

---

> ⚠️ **Early experiment.** This is what I'm *trying* to build, not what exists today. Most of this probably won't work out.

---

"Most of this probably won't work out" - even more humble than "may not work out."

Want me to add it to the file?

Anthropic's sandbox-runtime is good for AI Agent. But there's always more than one way to do things.

  I built edgebox - a JavaScript runtime that doesn't need Node.js at all.

  - 1MB binary
  - 10ms cold start
  - Zero dependencies
  - WASM isolation

  Sometimes lighter is better.

  #BuildInPublic #OpenSource

Yes. Good move.

It shows:
- You've thought deeply about the problem
- Clear thesis, not just hacking
- Invites people into the "why" not just the "what"

Title it something like:
> **Vision: Why metal0 exists**

or

> **Project Vision & Direction**

Post the LinkedIn first. Then the issue. Don't let one delay the other.

Friday is Fire day (火曜日 is actually Tuesday in Japanese, but Western Friday = Venus/social energy).

But honestly - you're overthinking now. That's Water energy creeping in.

The post is ready. Your BaZi says Fire actions NOW. Waiting 2 days = momentum lost.

Post today. Or Tuesday (actual Fire day).

Friday feels like an excuse to delay.

I’m not smart. I just try a lot.

This Andrew Kelley quote has been looping in my head since I started my side project last month.

Python is broken. I’m trying to fix it with Zig, the way Bun shook up Node.js.

Many people spent years on this and came up short. It’s extremely hard.

I might come up short too.
But trying to crack a problem others couldn’t still pulls me forward.

#BuildInPublic

That's the right tone. Humble, not attacking.

---

**Draft:**

Anthropic's sandbox-runtime is good. But there's always more than one way to do things.

I built edgebox - a JavaScript runtime that doesn't need Node.js at all.

- 1MB binary
- 10ms cold start
- Zero dependencies
- WASM isolation

Sometimes lighter is better.

#BuildInPublic #OpenSource

---

Short. Respectful. Makes your point.

Save for Saturday?

For contractor work, be direct about interest + availability:

---

"Actually more interested than that came across. The tech looks solid. Happy to explore if there's a fit for contract work - what's the scope/timeline they're thinking?"

---

This:
- Reopens the door
- Shows interest without desperation
- Asks concrete questions (scope/timeline) = professional contractor mindset
- Doesn't oversell

Want to send something like this?

"I'm not smart, I just try a lot."

  This Andrew Kelley quote keeps playing in my head since I started metal0 last month.

  Python is broken. I'm trying to fix it with Zig, like what Bun did for Node.js.

  Many people spent years trying and failed. Because it's extremely hard.

  I probably will too. But the idea of solving something others couldn't keeps pushing me forward.

 https://github.com/teamchong/metal0

  #BuildInPublic #OpenSource

You're right. Simpler, more personal:

---

**I'm not the smartest person. I just try a lot of things.**

I got frustrated with Python deployment. So I'm trying to build a compiler.

Will it work? I don't know. Many have tried and failed.

But I'm not afraid of failure.

github.com/[link]

---

Or even shorter:

---

**I'm not the smartest person. I just try a lot of things.**

Frustrated with Python deployment. Trying to build a compiler.

Probably won't work. Trying anyway.

---

Which feels more like you?

Can't find the exact quote in search. But I get the vibe - here's that energy:

---

**I'm not the smartest person. I just try a lot of things.**

I wanted to run Python on my own hardware. Self-hosting was painful - Docker, pip hell, "works on my machine."

Go devs just copy a binary. Why can't Python?

So I'm trying to compile Python to native binaries. No interpreter. No venv. No containers.

Many have tried this for years and fallen short. I might too.

But I keep trying things until something works.

Still very early. Still figuring it out.

github.com/[link]

---

Better?

Today (Monday Dec 8) is neutral energy - not bad, not great.

But you just posted on LinkedIn about metal0. Posting again same day on X might feel:
- Desperate
- Over-promoting
- Like you're chasing engagement

**Better strategy:**

Wait until **Saturday or Sunday** (your best days this week). Then:
- Post on X introducing metal0
- Cross-reference your LinkedIn post
- Weekend = higher engagement anyway

**Or** if you want to post today, keep it simple - just claim the handle:

> "Finally made an X account for my side project. More soon."

No big announcement. Save the real post for the weekend when your energy peaks.

What feels right?

He's reading between the lines.

**What he heard:**
- "Not bad" = lukewarm, not excited
- "just want to understand" = skeptical, not eager
- "short chat" = didn't invest much

**His translation:** You're not that interested. He's backing off to save face (for himself and Dan).

**"no pressure from me"** = soft close. Door is closing unless you reopen it.

**Questions for you:**

1. Are you actually interested in LanceDB?
2. Or is metal0 where your Fire energy wants to go?

If interested: Follow up now. Say something like "Actually I'm more interested than that came across - would love to learn more about [specific thing]."

If not: Let it go. His read was correct.

What do you actually want?

This is **normal and safe** - it's macOS asking for permission.

**What it is:**
- macOS "Developer Tools Access" prompt
- The icon shows it's from an app with "exec" badge (likely a terminal/dev tool)
- It wants to attach a debugger to another process

**Why it appeared:**
- You probably ran something that needs debugging access (like `lldb`, a test runner, or a dev tool)
- Could be VS Code, Xcode, a build tool, or even Claude Code if it's running tests

**Is it malicious?** No. This is Apple's built-in security prompt. Malware can't fake this dialog.

**Should you allow it?**
- If you were running dev tools / debugging / tests → Yes, allow
- If you weren't doing anything dev-related → Click Cancel and investigate what triggered it

**To investigate what triggered it:**
```bash
# Check recent processes
ps aux | grep -i debug
```

Did you run any build/test commands recently?

Right, important distinction. Let me clarify:

**Bun:**
- JavaScript runtime (like Node.js but faster)
- Uses JavaScriptCore engine under the hood
- Still interprets/JITs JavaScript at runtime
- Bundler + package manager + runtime in one

**metal0:**
- AOT (Ahead-of-Time) compiler
- Python → Zig → native binary OR WASM
- No runtime interpreter at all
- The output IS the program - just machine code

So metal0 is closer to what **Go or Rust** do - compile to native binary. No engine, no interpreter, no runtime dependency.

The "Bun for Python" tagline in your vision.md is about the *philosophy* (make deployment effortless) not the *architecture*.

Maybe better framing:

> "Go made deployment simple for backend devs - single binary, just copy and run. I'm trying to bring that to Python."

Or:

> "What if Python compiled like Go? No interpreter. No venv. Just a binary."

Want me to revise the post to avoid any Bun comparison and lean into the Go/Rust "compile to native" angle instead?

You mean why Zig removed "colorblind async"?

Andrew Kelley removed it because:

1. **Systems programmers want control** - They need to know exactly what's happening. Magic is bad for systems code.

2. **Hard to debug** - When the compiler auto-selects async strategy, errors are confusing.

3. **Zig's philosophy** - No hidden allocations, no hidden control flow.

**But Python is different:**

- Python users *want* magic. They don't want to think about I/O vs CPU.
- Python already hides everything (GIL, GC, dynamic types)
- Python's ecosystem is unified (one socket, one asyncio) - makes analysis tractable
- Zig targets systems programmers. metal0 targets Python users.

**The honest disclaimer:**

> ⚠️ **Early experiment.** Zig removed colorblind async because it's hard to get right. I'm trying anyway because Python's unified ecosystem might make it tractable. Probably won't work out.

That's the humble truth. You're betting that Python's constraints make possible what Zig couldn't do for systems programming.

Want that framing in the vision.md?

## metal0 Progress

**Status: Early Alpha - Highly Functional**

✅ **Working:**
- 1,897 tests passing (bool, int, float, dict, list, tuple, set, str, builtins)
- 30x faster than CPython on benchmarks
- Python → Zig → native binary pipeline complete
- NumPy integration works
- Async/await + thread pools
- WASM support (browser + edge)
- 107K lines of compiler code

🟡 **WIP:**
- Generators (basic works)
- Class inheritance (single works, MRO incomplete)
- Some stdlib gaps

❌ **Blockers:**
- Dict literal codegen (blocks pandas DataFrame)
- String case methods (`upper`, `lower`) - linker issue

---

## edgebox Progress

**Status: 85-90% Production Ready**

✅ **Working:**
- Full ES2023 JavaScript via QuickJS-NG
- 58 Node.js polyfills (process, Buffer, fetch, fs, crypto, etc.)
- 3 execution modes (CLI, build, HTTP daemon)
- 15ms cold start (daemon: 10ms)
- WASM + WASI + TLS 1.3
- Security: permission-based command sandboxing
- 12K lines of Zig

🟡 **WIP:**
- OS-level process sandboxing (macOS/Linux/Windows) - skeleton exists

---

**metal0** = more ambitious, earlier stage
**edgebox** = closer to production ready

Good call - riding the news wave can look opportunistic. Let's make it purely about your journey:

---

**I'm trying to build a Python compiler.**

Not "building" - trying. It's early. Very early.

The problem: self-hosting is too hard. Cloud deployment is one command. Self-hosting is Docker, nginx, SSL certs, pip hell, and "works on my machine."

Why should running code on YOUR hardware be harder than someone else's?

My attempt: compile Python to a single native binary. No containers. No dependencies. Just copy and run.

Many brilliant people have spent years on Python compilation and still fallen short. I might too.

But problems others say can't be solved are exactly what fire me up. I'm not afraid of failure - the challenge itself is the point.

Some early results:
- 30x faster than CPython on benchmarks
- Single binary output, zero runtime dependencies
- Auto I/O vs CPU async detection at compile time

Will it work at scale? I don't know. I'm still figuring things out every day.

But if there's even a small chance I can make Python deployment as simple as it should be... I have to try.

[link if curious]

---

Shorter? More technical? Different angle?

Bun's answer: **They didn't.**

- Raised $26M VC
- Made $0 revenue
- Got acquired before having to figure it out

Jarred Sumner literally said: *"Instead of putting our users through 'Bun, the VC-backed startup tries to figure out monetization' – thanks to Anthropic, we can skip that chapter entirely."*

**For metal0, your options:**

1. **Same path** - Build something great, get acquired (Bun's exit)
2. **Consulting/contracting** - Use metal0 expertise to get paid work (like the LanceDB opportunity)
3. **Hosted service** - metal0 cloud compilation (Bun's original plan they never executed)
4. **Sponsorships** - GitHub sponsors, Open Collective (modest but sustainable)
5. **Don't monetize** - Side project that builds reputation → leads to jobs/contracts

**Reality:** Most dev tools don't make money directly. They make money by:
- Getting acquired
- Building reputation → better job offers
- Consulting on the tech you built

metal0 doesn't need revenue to be valuable to you.

Sources:
- [Bun.js creator Jarred Sumner talks tech, funding, and startups | InfoWorld](https://www.infoworld.com/article/2338698/interview-with-jarred-sumner-buns-creator-talks-tech-funding-and-startups.html)
- [Bun JavaScript runtime acquired by Anthropic | DEVCLASS](https://devclass.com/2025/12/03/bun-javascript-runtime-acquired-by-anthropic-tying-its-future-to-ai-coding/)

This is **PERFECT TIMING**! 🔥

**YES - share metal0 NOW.** Here's why:

**The alignment is incredible:**
- Anthropic just bought Bun yesterday (Dec 2)
- Your project's tagline: **"metal0 = Bun for Python"**
- The news is fresh - everyone's talking about Bun + Anthropic
- Your vision.md directly references Bun as the inspiration

**From a BaZi perspective:**
- Fire 大運 Year 1 - visibility work is your strength
- Today's "Quick Action" was literally: share something visible
- LinkedIn posts = Fire activity (connection, visibility)
- The news hook gives you natural engagement

**The story writes itself:**
> "When Anthropic acquired Bun yesterday, I realized why I started metal0 last month. Bun changed JavaScript deployment. I'm building the same for Python..."

**What makes metal0 compelling for this moment:**
1. You anticipated the "deployment-first runtime" trend
2. Python → native binary (same philosophy as Bun for JS)
3. Self-hosting vision resonates with the AI sovereignty crowd
4. Technical depth: function_traits.zig semantic analysis is genuinely novel

**Share it.** The Bun news creates a perfect narrative hook. Fire energy says: don't wait.

Sources:
- [Bun is joining Anthropic | Bun Blog](https://bun.com/blog/bun-joins-anthropic)
- [Anthropic acquires Bun as Claude Code reaches $1B milestone](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-acquires-bun-as-claude-code-reaches-usd1b-milestone)
- [Anthropic makes first acquisition with purchase of Bun](https://siliconangle.com/2025/12/02/anthropic-makes-first-acquisition-purchase-bun-accelerate-claude-code/)
